name: grep-existing-annotations-before-parallel-subagent-dispatch
description: "Before parallel-subagent dispatch for bulk annotation work, grep for existing markers so agents don't waste cycles re-verifying already-applied annotations"

name: sandstorm-erosion-accumulator-buffer
description: Simulate progressive erosion/burial by accumulating wind deltas into a persistent height buffer sampled at render time.

name: twin-body-orbital-phase-offset
description: Render two orbiting bodies convincingly by phase-offsetting a shared sine driver rather than independent timers.

name: actuator-path-dual-compatibility
description: Actuator endpoints must respond at both /actuator/** and /actuator/{appName}/** for deployment compatibility.

name: batch-processing-vs-single-file
description: Python API has both identify_path() (single file) and identify_paths() (batch) — batch is optimized for throughput.

name: claude-code-routines-cloud-automation-model
description: Claude Code Routines (research preview) are saved cloud sessions — prompt + repos + connectors + environment — triggered by schedule, HTTP POST, or GitHub events. One routine can combine all three. Daily run cap per account; branch pushes restricted to `claude/*` by default.

name: claude-session-resume-semantics
description: If claude --resume <id> doesn't find the session, Claude silently generates a fresh session and exits. Treat emitted_id != requested_id AND failed status as "resume didn't land".

name: claude-stream-json-protocol
description: Claude Code CLI's stream-json mode emits typed JSON events (assistant, user, system, result, log) that wrap content blocks like text/thinking/tool_use/tool_result.

name: cli-release-via-git-tag
description: CLI releases are cut from semver tags on main — push a tag, GitHub Actions runs tests, GoReleaser builds multi-platform binaries and updates the Homebrew tap.

name: confid-vs-resourceid-routing
description: Queries support both `configIds` (tenant/policy scope) and `resourceId` (agent scope); filter routes to different Mongo match fields

name: device-revocation-status-codes-contract
description: Client-server contract used by PowerSync token/upload endpoints to signal account and device state. Codes are load-be...

name: endpoint-service-dual-pattern
description: Two coexisting API service patterns — factory functions (apmServices) and direct functional exports (alarmService) — with centralized endpoint constants

name: es-xpack-transport-vs-rest-ssl
description: Elasticsearch transport SSL and REST SSL are independent X-Pack configs; enabling one does not enable the other

name: kafka-avro-change-flag-contract
description: Every Configuration-domain Avro event carries an `actionType` enum (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) plus boolean change flags per mutable section (e.g. `parentChanged`, `tagFiltersChanged`, `configurationsChanged`) rather than full before/after payloads.

name: kafka-organization-id-record-header
description: Place organizationId (tenant) in the Kafka record header, not only in the payload — downstream consumers route by header before deserializing.

name: live-chart-timestamp-normalization
description: LIVE charts accept `normalizeTimestamp` + `normalizeIntervalMs` to align per-agent millisecond offsets into a single x-axis

name: no-dereference-option-symlinks
description: no_dereference=True (--no-dereference in CLI) treats symlinks as type 'symlink' instead of following them.

name: orchestration-command-event-and-receipt-contract
description: Client sends commands -> server emits events and receipts -> client observes state updates and completion signals

name: origin-whitelist-atomic-hot-swap
description: Store the WebSocket allowed-origins list in an atomic.Value so it can be hot-swapped without locking the request path, with env-based defaults for dev.

name: overwrite-reason-field
description: MagikaPrediction.overwrite_reason indicates why the final label differs from the raw model prediction.

name: pat-vs-jwt-endpoint-parity
description: Same API endpoints accept both session JWTs (browser) and personal access tokens (CLI/daemon) — the auth middleware dispatches by prefix and treats the two uniformly downstream.

name: runtime-mode-approval-policy-and-sandbox-mode
description: T3 Code has a global runtime mode that controls whether commands need approval and what filesystem access is allowed

name: slug-first-id-fallback-resolver
description: Resolve multi-tenant identity from slug first (header or query), fall back to UUID for CLI/daemon/legacy clients, collapse "invalid slug" distinctly from "no identifier" for proper 400 vs 404 responses.

name: stomp-heartbeat-10s-bidirectional
description: STOMP broker heartbeat set to 10s/10s (client↔server) is a reasonable default for detecting dead WebSocket sessions within ~20s

name: stream-seeking-and-position-preservation
description: identify_stream() seeks around the stream for feature extraction but restores the original position before returning.

name: timeselector-mode-interval-range-mapping
description: Clients send a `mode` enum (RAW..YEAR); the server maps it to a (bin-interval, query-range) pair and picks the right pre-aggregated collection

name: verification-code-attempts-rate-limit
description: Track verification-code attempts per-code and lock out after N tries; protect against brute-force of 6-digit codes.

name: ws-token-never-in-url
description: Never put auth tokens in WebSocket URL query params — proxies, CDNs, and browser history will log them. Use cookies or first-message auth instead.

name: xlog-extended-transaction-trace-with-compression
description: XLog is scouter's per-request transaction trace format — hierarchical typed steps stored as compressed binary, indexed by end-time, with flat metadata for server-side filtering

name: zod-openapi-schema-conventions
description: Use `@hono/zod-openapi`'s `z` (not `zod` directly), derive types with `z.infer<typeof X>`, co-locate route schemas per domain, and derive enum constants from `schema.options` instead of duplicating arrays.

name: appimage-fuse-chrome-sandbox-incompatibility
description: Electron's `chrome-sandbox` requires the setuid-root bit (permissions 4755) to create unprivileged user namespaces; AppImages mount via FUSE as a non-root user, which prevents the sandbox from functioning — the workaround is `--no-sandbox` in the AppImage launcher.

name: appimage-zsync-delta-update-metadata
description: Locally-built AppImages lack the `.zsync` metadata file required for binary delta updates; only AppImages published via GitHub Releases with the correct metadata (embedded `update` field) get efficient delta updates through tools like AppImageUpdate.

name: additive-registry-schema-versioning
description: "JSON registry 진화는 version bump + 새 키 empty container + 첫 쓰기 시 자동 마이그레이션으로 하라"

name: agent-backend-unified-interface
description: Unified Go interface for driving multiple coding-agent CLIs (Claude, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Hermes, Gemini, Pi, Cursor) behind one Execute/Session/Result contract.

name: agent-mcp-config-via-temp-file
description: Pass MCP server config to a coding-agent CLI via a temp JSON file (--mcp-config <path>), not inline — controls which MCP servers the spawned agent can see, independent of the parent session.

name: agent-memory-binding-vs-recall
description: Agent memory systems that ace retrieval benchmarks still fail operationally because stored skills aren't *bound* to the episodes that validated them. Fix is a memory bundle — skill record + episode record + bidirectional link — returned together on recall.

name: annotation-driven-authz-via-function-id
description: Controllers declare required capability with a method-level enum annotation; an interceptor enforces it against JWT-derived roles

name: archon-deterministic-ai-coding-harness
description: Archon enforces deterministic AI-assisted coding by encoding workflows as YAML DAGs — deterministic nodes (tests, git ops) bracket AI-driven nodes (plan, implement, review), each run isolated in its own git worktree. The pattern generalizes beyond Archon itself.

name: assumeutxo-snapshot-chainstate-phases
description: (Blockchain-specific) Multi-chainstate snapshot validation — active chainstate syncs from a trusted-but-unverified UTXO snapshot while a background chainstate validates the snapshot's base, with a phased state machine for clean cutover.

name: bilingual-user-facing-strings
description: A bilingual_str struct carrying both original and translated forms lets GUI show translated text while logs/stderr keep the original — no locale branching in business code.

name: boilerplate-generated-business-logic-manual
description: Split AI codegen scope along the boilerplate/business-logic line — AI produces skeletons, humans fill domain rules

name: chi-router-for-go-apis
description: go-chi/chi is the router of choice for REST APIs with middleware groups, URL params, and nested routing — simpler than gin/echo while keeping idiomatic net/http compatibility.

name: claude-plugin-skill-md-is-sufficient
description: "스킬 = SKILL.md 하나. 코드, 빌드, 의존성, 스크립트 없이도 완전히 동작한다."

name: cli-implemented-in-rust-for-speed
description: The Magika CLI is Rust, not Python, to eliminate interpreter startup overhead.

name: commons-logging-exclusion-strategy
description: Globally exclude commons-logging, log4j, and slf4j-log4j12 in Gradle so SLF4J+Logback is the only logging backend on the classpath

name: config-minimization-min-json
description: Model configs ship as config.min.json (minified) to shrink package size — especially important for browser deployment.

name: content-types-knowledge-base-minified
description: The full content-types KB ships as content_types_kb.min.json so APIs can offer rich metadata without a large overhead.

name: cutmix-augmentation-v1-to-v3
description: CutMix data augmentation was used in v1, removed in v2_1, and reintroduced in v3_1.

name: daemon-per-profile-isolation
description: Multiple daemon profiles on one host — production, staging, dev worktrees — each with its own config dir, PID, health port, and workspace root.

name: daemon-task-polling-architecture
description: Local agent daemon registers runtimes per workspace, polls server for claimed tasks, spawns agent CLI in an isolated workspace dir, streams results back.

name: data-patch-package-for-migrations
description: One-off data patches (backfills, embedded→referenced splits, field additions) are grouped under a `patch` package so they're isolated from day-to-day service code and easy to audit/remove after a release.

name: devtools-frontend-reused-as-library
description: The project imports pieces of Chrome DevTools Frontend (`TraceEngine`, `PerformanceTraceFormatter`, `HeapSnapshotModel`, `IssuesManager`, `CrUXManager`) as a library dependency, so tool output matches what users see in the real DevTools panels.

name: domain-registry-plus-projects-yaml
description: Central domain registry + per-user path map for multi-repo codegen

name: dual-design-token-pipeline-arch
description: Project runs two parallel design token pipelines — legacy SD v3→SCSS for sirius/Ant Design and modern SD v5→CSS @theme for AI Portal/Tailwind v4

name: dual-redis-lock-vs-access-control
description: "The service connects to two separate Redis instances: Lock Control (default port 6399) and Access Control (default port 6389). They are not interchangeable."

name: duckdb-vectorized-analytical-execution
description: DuckDB's design has five separable pillars — vectorized execution, pipelined operators, explicit memory + grouped aggregation, ART indexing, and a dedicated query rewriter — distinguishing it from row-at-a-time OLTP engines.

name: engineering-principles-archon
description: Six-principle engineering charter — KISS / YAGNI / DRY+Rule-of-Three / SRP+ISP / Fail-Fast / No-Autonomous-Lifecycle-Mutation / Determinism / Reversibility — applied as implementation constraints, not slogans.

name: env-override-last-wins-chain
description: Environment variables resolve across four sources with "last wins" precedence.

name: event-driven-init-wait-for-services
description: Initialization work is deferred until leader status and dependent-service readiness are confirmed via a `@WaitForServices` event listener

name: fail-fast-explicit-errors-ai-runtime
description: In an AI-agent runtime, silent fallback and swallowed errors are extra-dangerous — they can broaden permissions, retry forever, or charge the user without warning. Prefer throwing early with a clear message.

name: gradle-avro-plugin-config
description: davidmc24 avro-gradle-plugin config — createSetters=false + fieldVisibility=PRIVATE enforces immutable generated event schemas

name: hierarchical-agents-md-catalog
description: "루트 AGENTS.md(프로젝트 개요) + `skills/AGENTS.md`(스킬 카탈로그) 2계층 구조. 하위는 상위를 `<!-- Parent: ../AGENTS.md -->` 주석으로 가리킨다."

name: in-page-tool-bridge-via-custom-event
description: Web pages can expose their own tools to the automation agent by listening for a `devtoolstooldiscovery` CustomEvent and calling `event.respondWith(toolGroup)`; the MCP server discovers them via CDP `DOMDebugger.getEventListeners` and bridges each to MCP tools.

name: interface-abstraction-for-modularity
description: Pure-virtual C++ interfaces in a dedicated src/interfaces/ directory decouple modules without symbol-level coupling, and enable IPC code generation later.

name: internal-packages-raw-source-pattern
description: Shared monorepo packages export raw .ts/.tsx files (no pre-compilation); consuming app bundlers compile them directly.

name: jar-task-disabled-bootjar-only
description: Gradle `jar` task is disabled so only `bootJar` produces an artifact — CI/Docker consumers never receive an ambiguous plain JAR

name: jvm-module-opens-java21-spring-kafka
description: Java 21 runs Spring Boot + Kafka/MongoDB clients under strict module access; two --add-opens flags are required or reflective serializers fail at runtime.

name: k-git-tag-registry-versioning
description: "Git tag를 semver registry로 쓰면 별도 version manifest 없이 rollback·pinning을 얻는다"

name: kafka-1h-topic-and-nonmetric-pool
description: Raw alarm path uses a 1-hour-retention Kafka topic and a dedicated non-metric thread pool — isolates alarm latency from batch ingest

name: kafka-avro-schedule-event-chain
description: Collection scheduling decouples Kafka ingress from per-DBMS handlers via four typed BlockingQueues

name: kafka-avro-schema-registry-env-topology
description: Kafka uses Avro + Confluent Schema Registry; partition and replication counts are environment-driven.

name: kafka-partition-topology-design
description: Asymmetric Kafka partition counts per topic (high for hot-path, default for admin topics) match volume and concurrency needs without over-provisioning

name: live-mode-aggregation-exceptions
description: LIVE mode reads raw data by default, but equalizer / multi-resource / TopN charts still aggregate raw at query time

name: llm-tool-calling-mxn-wire-format-problem
description: Open-source LLMs each encode tool calls in a different wire format (special tokens, XML, channel notation, JSON blocks), so M inference apps × N models = M×N parsers. Fix is declarative tool-call specs shipped with the model and consumed by both grammar engines and parsers.

name: lucida-framework-gate-annotations
description: Internal framework (lucida-framework-*) uses opt-in annotations (@EnabledMessage, @EnableDataExport, @EnableSchedule, @EnableCommand) on the Application class to gate auto-config; omitting one silently disables the feature.

name: lucida-ui-design-token-reference
description: "lucida-ui 프로젝트의 실제 디자인 토큰 값 참조표 — 팔레트, 시맨틱, 사이즈, 타이포, 컴포넌트 스타일"

name: magika-sampling-strategy
description: Magika samples beginning, middle, and end blocks of a file rather than processing all bytes.

name: mcp-stdio-child-of-daemon-topology
description: The chrome-devtools-mcp daemon architecture is a 3-process chain — CLI client talks to a daemon over a socket, and the daemon spawns the real MCP stdio server as a child and connects to it with an MCP Client. This keeps "MCP stdio transport" as the only server protocol while adding CLI-level persistence.

name: mf-cross-remote-import-forbidden
description: In a Module Federation monorepo with per-remote webpack aliases, remotes must not import from sibling remotes directly — share code through `shared/` or through MF exposes.

name: mf-expose-requires-memoryrouter-wrapper
description: Components exposed via Module Federation that use react-router hooks must be wrapped in MemoryRouter at the expose boundary, not BrowserRouter and not a full-app router wrapper.

name: mf-shared-singleton-config
description: 227 shared dependencies configured as Webpack Module Federation singletons with strict versioning; react-router-dom intentionally excluded

name: model-api-contract-sync-and-async
description: Rust API exposes both SyncInput and AsyncInput traits so callers can pick blocking or async without two libraries.

name: model-generation-tooling-in-rust-gen
description: rust/gen/ contains code generators for new model versions — sync.sh and publish.sh automate the propagation.

name: model-not-used-for-edge-cases
description: The deep learning model is intentionally bypassed for empty files, directories, symlinks, and very small files (<8 bytes).

name: model-variants-standard-fast-begonly
description: Magika ships standard / fast / begonly model variants with different speed-vs-accuracy tradeoffs.

name: mongodb-aggregated-stats-as-views
description: Precomputed hour/day statistics exposed as MongoDB read-only views instead of physical collections to simplify reads and keep them consistent with source

name: multi-domain-split-via-domain-mapping
description: Split multi-domain screens via a domain-mapping manifest — one OpenAPI file per owning domain

name: multi-layer-css-architecture
description: Four-layer CSS architecture — SCSS (sirius design system), LESS (Ant Design vars), Tailwind v4 (AI Portal with scope isolation), plain CSS — each with distinct webpack rules and toolchains

name: multiprocess-architecture-via-ipc
description: Decompose a monolith into cooperating processes by defining interfaces in a neutral schema language (Cap'n Proto), then codegen client/server proxies that preserve the original C++ interface shape.

name: nfs-backed-volumes-for-docker-swarm
description: Cross-node Docker Swarm persistence via a single NFS share mounted at the same host path on every swarm node

name: no-autonomous-lifecycle-mutation
description: A process that cannot reliably distinguish "actively running elsewhere" from "orphaned by a crash" must not autonomously mark work as failed/cancelled/abandoned based on a timer or staleness guess.

name: oh-my-claudecode-ai-slop-cleaner
description: "OMC implements \"ai-slop-cleaner\" as: Clean AI-generated code slop with a regression-safe, deletion-first workflow and optional reviewer-only mode"

name: oh-my-claudecode-ask
description: "OMC implements \"ask\" as: Process-first advisor routing for Claude, Codex, or Gemini via `omc ask`, with artifact capture and no raw CLI assembly"

name: oh-my-claudecode-autopilot
description: "OMC implements \"autopilot\" as: Full autonomous execution from idea to working code"

name: oh-my-claudecode-cancel
description: "OMC implements \"cancel\" as: Cancel any active OMC mode (autopilot, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, swarm, ultrapilot, pipeline, team)"

name: oh-my-claudecode-ccg
description: "OMC implements \"ccg\" as: Claude-Codex-Gemini tri-model orchestration via /ask codex + /ask gemini, then Claude synthesizes results"

name: oh-my-claudecode-configure-notifications
description: "OMC implements \"configure-notifications\" as: Configure notification integrations (Telegram, Discord, Slack) via natural language"

name: oh-my-claudecode-debug
description: "OMC implements \"debug\" as: Diagnose the current OMC session or repo state using logs, traces, state, and focused reproduction"

name: oh-my-claudecode-deep-dive
description: "OMC implements \"deep-dive\" as: 2-stage pipeline: trace (causal investigation) -> deep-interview (requirements crystallization) with 3-point injection"

name: oh-my-claudecode-deep-interview
description: "OMC implements \"deep-interview\" as: Socratic deep interview with mathematical ambiguity gating before autonomous execution"

name: oh-my-claudecode-deepinit
description: "OMC implements \"deepinit\" as: Deep codebase initialization with hierarchical AGENTS.md documentation"

name: oh-my-claudecode-external-context
description: "OMC implements \"external-context\" as: Invoke parallel document-specialist agents for external web searches and documentation lookup"

name: oh-my-claudecode-hud
description: "OMC implements \"hud\" as: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements)"

name: oh-my-claudecode-learner
description: "OMC implements \"learner\" as: Extract a learned skill from the current conversation"

name: oh-my-claudecode-mcp-setup
description: "OMC implements \"mcp-setup\" as: Configure popular MCP servers for enhanced agent capabilities"

name: oh-my-claudecode-omc-doctor
description: "OMC implements \"omc-doctor\" as: Diagnose and fix oh-my-claudecode installation issues"

name: oh-my-claudecode-omc-reference
description: "OMC implements \"omc-reference\" as: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills."

name: oh-my-claudecode-omc-setup
description: "OMC implements \"omc-setup\" as: Install or refresh oh-my-claudecode for plugin, npm, and local-dev setups from the canonical setup flow"

name: oh-my-claudecode-omc-teams
description: "OMC implements \"omc-teams\" as: CLI-team runtime for claude, codex, or gemini workers in tmux panes when you need process-based parallel execution"

name: oh-my-claudecode-plan
description: "OMC implements \"plan\" as: Strategic planning with optional interview workflow"

name: oh-my-claudecode-project-session-manager
description: "OMC implements \"project-session-manager\" as: Worktree-first dev environment manager for issues, PRs, and features with optional tmux sessions"

name: oh-my-claudecode-ralph
description: "OMC implements \"ralph\" as: Self-referential loop until task completion with configurable verification reviewer"

name: oh-my-claudecode-ralplan
description: "OMC implements \"ralplan\" as: Consensus planning entrypoint that auto-gates vague ralph/autopilot/team requests before execution"

name: oh-my-claudecode-release
description: "OMC implements \"release\" as: Generic release assistant — analyzes repo release rules, caches them in .omc/RELEASE_RULE.md, then guides the release"

name: oh-my-claudecode-remember
description: "OMC implements \"remember\" as: Review reusable project knowledge and decide what belongs in project memory, notepad, or durable docs"

name: oh-my-claudecode-sciomc
description: "OMC implements \"sciomc\" as: Orchestrate parallel scientist agents for comprehensive analysis with AUTO mode"

name: oh-my-claudecode-self-improve
description: "OMC implements \"self-improve\" as: Autonomous evolutionary code improvement engine with tournament selection"

name: oh-my-claudecode-setup
description: "OMC implements \"setup\" as: Use first for install/update routing — sends setup, doctor, or MCP requests to the correct OMC setup flow"

name: oh-my-claudecode-skill
description: "OMC implements \"skill\" as: Manage local skills - list, add, remove, search, edit, setup wizard"

name: oh-my-claudecode-skillify
description: "OMC implements \"skillify\" as: Turn a repeatable workflow from the current session into a reusable OMC skill draft"

name: oh-my-claudecode-team
description: "OMC implements \"team\" as: N coordinated agents on shared task list using Claude Code native teams"

name: oh-my-claudecode-trace
description: "OMC implements \"trace\" as: Evidence-driven tracing lane that orchestrates competing tracer hypotheses in Claude built-in team mode"

name: oh-my-claudecode-ultraqa
description: "OMC implements \"ultraqa\" as: QA cycling workflow - test, verify, fix, repeat until goal met"

name: oh-my-claudecode-ultrawork
description: "OMC implements \"ultrawork\" as: Parallel execution engine for high-throughput task completion"

name: oh-my-claudecode-verify
description: "OMC implements \"verify\" as: Verify that a change really works before you claim completion"

name: oh-my-claudecode-visual-verdict
description: "OMC implements \"visual-verdict\" as: Structured visual QA verdict for screenshot-to-reference comparisons"

name: oh-my-claudecode-wiki
description: "OMC implements \"wiki\" as: LLM Wiki — persistent markdown knowledge base that compounds across sessions (Karpathy model)"

name: oh-my-claudecode-writer-memory
description: "OMC implements \"writer-memory\" as: Agentic memory system for writers - track characters, relationships, scenes, and themes"

name: openapi-as-single-fe-be-contract
description: Use OpenAPI 3.0 as the only intermediate artifact between planning and codegen; both FE and BE generators consume the same file

name: package-boundary-hard-rules
description: Monorepo package boundaries are hard constraints — violating them breaks cross-platform architecture.

name: pdl-launch-attribute-vs-cluster-size-interaction
description: `cudaLaunchAttributeClusterDimension` (for thread-block clusters) and `cudaLaunchAttributeProgrammaticStreamSerializa...

name: per-content-type-threshold-system
description: Magika gates predictions with per-content-type confidence thresholds (range ~0.5–0.95), not a single global threshold.

name: per-tool-serialization-via-mutex
description: Every incoming MCP tool call in chrome-devtools-mcp acquires a single global `toolMutex` before its handler runs, serializing all tool executions on the shared browser context to avoid CDP concurrency hazards.

name: plugin-architecture-runtime-vs-startup-loading
description: Scouter server loads hot-reloadable script plugins (.plug files) for alerts/filters and compiled JAR plugins for stateful data handlers (sinks, connection-pool-heavy integrations)

name: polymorphic-assignees-member-or-agent
description: An issue's assignee can be a human member OR an AI agent; stored as (assignee_type, assignee_id) tuple and rendered with distinct styling.

name: polymorphic-metadata-via-enum-switch
description: Store heterogeneous domain payloads under one field as raw Document, then deserialize with Gson chosen by an enum discriminator

name: port-out-no-optional-raise-in-adapter
description: In hexagonal codegen, single-record lookups should raise a domain exception inside the Adapter rather than return `Optional<T>` from the Port-Out interface

name: provider-contract-layer-zero-sdk-deps
description: The interfaces that a workflow engine uses to talk to AI providers must live in a file with zero SDK imports and zero runtime side effects, so downstream packages don't inherit SDK deps.

name: published-vs-realtime-view-pages
description: PublishedViewPage renders a frozen deploy snapshot; RealTimeViewPage renders the live editable dashboard

name: random-snippet-selection-augmentation
description: Random Snippet Selection (RSS) augmentation extracts variable-length snippets from training samples — important for textual content types.

name: scouter-three-tier-architecture
description: Scouter APM enforces strict agent → server → client separation, with an optional standalone HTTP webapp for horizontal scaling of query load

name: scss-css-variable-theme-system
description: Architecture decision — SCSS + CSS custom properties + data-theme attribute for dark/light theming in a large React monorepo

name: seekable-abstraction-for-io
description: Python uses a Seekable wrapper to unify bytes / paths / streams under one feature-extraction codepath.

name: server-state-vs-client-state-split
description: TanStack Query owns all server state; Zustand owns all client state. Never duplicate server data into a store.

name: shallow-clone-mktemp-cleanup-pattern
description: "publish/install/원격 목록 조회처럼 원격 git repo를 만질 때는 `mktemp -d → git clone --depth 1 → 작업 → rm -rf`. 작업 디렉토리를 오염시키지 않는다."

name: sirius-component-taxonomy
description: Sirius design system (@nkia/sirius) organizes 200+ components into 5 categories — data-display, data-entry, feedback, layout, navigation

name: sirius-wraps-antd-design-system
description: Sirius design system wraps Ant Design components instead of replacing them — rationale, extension pattern, and upgrade implications

name: storage-adapter-for-platform-neutrality
description: Shared packages depend on a StorageAdapter interface, not on localStorage — each platform injects its own adapter (browser localStorage, Electron store, in-memory for tests).

name: swap-ab-for-m-grouped-on-sm100
description: On Blackwell (SM100), every M-grouped GEMM variant (contiguous, masked, contiguous-with-psum) unconditionally enables...

name: synthetic-utf8-datasets-v3-3
description: standard_v3_3 added synthetic UTF-8 datasets (non-ASCII text, JSON) to improve international/multilingual handling.

name: tenant-context-per-request-interceptor
description: Extract tenant id from JWT/header in preHandle, store in ThreadLocal, clear in afterCompletion to isolate multi-tenant data access

name: tensorflow-js-for-browser-deployment
description: The web demo uses a TensorFlow.js model for on-device, browser-based inference (no server round trip).

name: wait-for-services-eureka-startup-init
description: Defer schema/index/data bootstrap until named services report ready in discovery, with bounded exponential backoff and leader election

name: widget-is-frozen-group-snapshot
description: Widgets are snapshots of a group at save time; later edits to the source group do not propagate

name: workdir-preserved-for-retry
description: Per-task agent workdirs are intentionally NOT deleted after task completion — they enable faster retries (cached repo state, warmed caches) and post-mortem inspection.

name: ws-invalidation-not-store-writes
description: WebSocket event handlers invalidate React Query cache keys — they never write directly to Zustand stores or the cache.

name: yorkie-team-server-split
description: Yorkie holds live canvas state; the team server holds metadata (projects, assets, datasources) — never mix

name: agent-loop-architecture-reference
description: Minimal ~100-line reference architecture for an LLM agent runner loop with generator-based streaming, StepOutcome exit semantics, and periodic tool-schema reset.

name: agent-native-software-philosophy
description: Craft Agents' "Agent Native" premise — describe what you want, let the agent figure out how, and treat customization itself as a prompt rather than a code change.

name: built-in-session-tools-parity
description: Session-scoped tools (SubmitPlan, config_validate) live in session-tools-core and are wired identically into Claude and Pi backends via separate stdio MCP servers — parity tested by claude/session-tool-parity.test.ts and pi/session-tool-parity.test.ts.

name: dual-backend-claude-and-pi
description: Why Craft Agents runs two AgentBackend implementations (Claude SDK + Pi SDK) side by side instead of a single abstraction — different provider ergonomics, auth flows, and model capabilities.

name: dynamic-uv-python-scripts
description: Craft Agents ships a bundled uv binary and a set of PEP-723 self-dependency Python scripts (pdf_tool, docx_tool, xlsx_tool, markitdown, ical_tool, ...) so document-handling tools work without asking the user to install Python.

name: effect-ts-layer-dependency-injection-for-testing
description: Effect.ts Layers provide compile-time dependency injection; compose layers to swap real services with test stubs

name: gep-asset-model
description: Evolver's GEP (Genome Evolution Protocol) stores evolution state as three on-disk assets — reusable Genes, success Capsules, and an append-only EvolutionEvents log — so every change is auditable and the selector can score candidates by signal match.

name: gep-genome-evolution-protocol
description: Protocol for tracking and applying genome mutations as evolution genes and capsules

name: hermes-agent-project-structure
description: Reference layout for a tool-heavy LLM agent framework with CLI, gateway, TUI, ACP, and RL environments.

name: interceptor-cjs-preload-pattern
description: The unified-network-interceptor is a TS file bundled to CJS with esbuild and loaded into every SDK subprocess via --require/--preload to patch globalThis.fetch before the SDK captures it — the only reliable way to observe/modify LLM HTTP traffic.

name: investigation-state-shape
description: OpenSRE's investigation state is a TypedDict with ~30 keys spanning auth (org_id, user_id), alert (raw_alert, alert_name, severity, is_noise), routing (alert_source, kube_namespace), planning (planned_actions, plan_audit, available_action_names), evidence (the dict tools fill), diagnosis (root_cause, validated_claims, validity_score), masking (masking_map), and loop control (investigation_loop_count, investigation_recommendations).

name: kronos-decoder-only-tokenizer-plus-transformer
description: Kronos factors the model into a BSQ tokenizer (encoder+decoder Transformer) plus a decoder-only causal Transformer with hierarchical dual-head output.

name: large-response-summarization-flow
description: When a tool result exceeds ~60KB (15k tokens), Craft Agents saves it to sessionDir/long_responses, runs Haiku summarization with the tool's _intent as context, and returns summary + file path to the LLM — the UX win is the agent keeps context while the dev saves tokens.

name: local-proxy-auth-isolation
description: A local-HTTP proxy process owns all hub credentials and sync state; the business agent only speaks to `127.0.0.1`. Credential rotation, retries, and offline mode become the proxy's problem, not the agent's.

name: mcp-pool-vs-direct-connections
description: Why Craft Agents centralizes MCP source connections in a McpClientPool exposed as a single Streamable-HTTP endpoint to SDK subprocesses, instead of letting each subprocess connect independently.

name: opensre-investigation-pipeline-architecture
description: The OpenSRE agent splits an SRE investigation into six discrete LangGraph nodes (extract → resolve → plan → investigate → diagnose → publish) with a bounded plan-investigate-diagnose loop, dual chat/investigation entry modes, and per-investigation masking that survives state transitions.

name: permission-modes-as-product-primitive
description: Craft Agents treats three permission modes (explore/ask/execute, internal safe/ask/allow-all) as a first-class product primitive — cycled by SHIFT+TAB, fired as bus events, stored per-session, with a CompiledBashPattern allowlist driving Explore.

name: priority-lower-is-higher-stable-sort
description: Common registry pitfall — priority numbers where 0 = highest (tried first), 10 = lowest. Pick one convention, document it loudly, and combine with stable sort so insertion order breaks ties.

name: protocol-version-backward-compat
description: Client/server handshake exchanges PROTOCOL_VERSION from shared/protocol; version mismatches produce a dedicated PROTOCOL_VERSION_UNSUPPORTED error rather than generic connection failure.

name: proxy-lifecycle-with-authentication
description: Local Proxy lifecycle — startup, authentication, sync loop, graceful shutdown

name: session-as-jsonl-append-only
description: Rationale for storing agent sessions as append-only JSONL (header line + one message per line) rather than a single JSON blob — fast append writes, cheap header-only scans, atomic message semantics.

name: session-sharing-viewer-design
description: Craft Agents ships a separate apps/viewer Vite app that renders read-only session snapshots shared via agents.craft.do/s/:id — a published-session artifact export with no backend dependency at view time.

name: task-receiver-async-work-queueing
description: Async work queue for receiving tasks from hub, applying updates, managing task state

name: thin-client-vs-embedded-server
description: Craft Agents Electron app runs in two modes depending on whether CRAFT_SERVER_URL is set — embedded mode starts server in-process, thin-client mode connects to remote.

name: traceability-registry
description: Traceability registry YAML mapping technical requirements to architecture decisions

name: unified-entry-dispatch-by-uri-scheme
description: One public convert() entry point that branches on URI scheme (file:/data:/http[s]:) and input type (Path, bytes, BinaryIO, requests.Response) into specialized convert_local / convert_uri / convert_stream / convert_response methods.

name: websocket-as-execution-boundary-in-desktop-and-web
description: T3 Code uses WebSocket as the clean architectural boundary between browser/desktop UI and server runtime (git, providers, terminals)

name: webui-http-handler-composed-with-ws
description: The WsRpcServer accepts an optional httpHandler that serves the web UI + /api/auth on the SAME port as the WebSocket upgrades — one binding, one certificate, two client kinds authenticated with the same token.

name: why-bun-not-node
description: Why Craft Agents standardized on Bun for the server + CLI but keeps Electron on Node — startup speed, single-file bundling for subprocesses, TypeScript without build step, but Electron can't swap its renderer runtime.

name: wsrpcserver-push-target-model
description: pushTyped(wsServer, channel, target, workspaceId, payload) uses a PushTarget union ({ to: 'workspace' } | { to: 'client'; clientId } | { to: 'broadcast' }) so the server can efficiently unicast, workspace-broadcast, or global-broadcast a single serialized envelope.

name: audiovae-v2-asymmetric-rationale
description: AudioVAE V2 encodes at 16kHz for compute efficiency but decodes to 48kHz via learned upsampling, eliminating external resamplers

name: vad-silence-trim-optional
description: VoxCPM optionally trims leading/trailing silence from reference audio using librosa frame-wise RMS below a top_db threshold

name: permission-system-design-peer-config
description: Permissions stored per-peer in Config::get_peer_option(); not global; sync'd via the rendezvous server.

name: chrome-extension-based-web-automation-via-cdp
description: Browser automation via Chrome extension + CDP bridge that preserves the user's real login state — Selenium-free, Playwright-free, survives autofill and closed shadow DOM.

name: playwright-stealth-cloudflare-bypass
description: Playwright can bypass Cloudflare JS challenges by injecting init scripts that hide the `webdriver` property and fake `navigator.platform`/`navigator.vendor`; use `wait_for_timeout` instead of navigation timeout to capture download redirect URLs.

name: scheduler-and-cron-task-execution-model
description: File-based lightweight cron alternative — JSON task definitions, report-timestamp cooldown tracking, health-check introspection, max-delay guardrails.

name: debug-vs-release-build-differences
description: "Release: optimized codecs + LTO. Debug: flexi_logger on desktop, android_logger on Android."

name: protobuf-code-generation-build-rs
description: Protobuf codegen runs in build.rs via prost; generated types auto-derive Serialize/Deserialize for FFI reuse.

name: python-build-script-usage
description: build.py wraps cross-platform builds with --flutter, --hwcodec, --vram, --release flags.

name: vcpkg-dependency-management
description: C++ deps (libvpx, libyuv, opus, aom) via vcpkg; set VCPKG_ROOT; custom Sciter library download.

name: bun-auto-loads-cwd-env
description: Bun unconditionally loads `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.development`, `.env.production` from the CWD before user code runs — regardless of framework.

name: bun-compile-freezes-import-meta
description: Inside a `bun build --compile` binary, `import.meta.url` and `import.meta.dir` are frozen to the build host's filesystem path and point to nothing on the user's machine.

name: ci-gpg-signing-batch-mode-non-interactive
description: GPG signing in CI requires `--batch --yes` flags, a pre-imported key, and `git config user.signingKey` set; without a TTY, pinentry will fail and the signing step hangs or errors unless all interactive prompts are bypassed.

name: ci-push-race-condition-retry-rebase-loop
description: Parallel CI jobs pushing to the same branch (e.g., `gh-pages`) race; solve with a `git pull --rebase` + retry loop with exponential backoff, or serialize jobs with `needs:` dependencies to avoid the race entirely.

name: github-variables-version-management
description: Storing version strings in GitHub Actions repository variables (not committed files) avoids merge conflicts on automated version bumps and provides a single source of truth accessible to both CI jobs and local scripts via the `gh` CLI.

name: legacy-vs-explicit-subcommand-migration
description: VoxCPM migrated from implicit mode detection (arg presence) to explicit subcommands (voxcpm design|clone|batch); old style emits deprecation warning

name: async-clipboard-race-condition
description: Clipboard access retries (3x at 33ms intervals) to handle concurrent-access conflicts on Windows.

name: clipboard-context-lazy-initialization
description: Clipboard context is lazy-init on first check_clipboard(); one context per side.

name: clipboard-ownership-protocol
description: Clipboard-ownership detection uses a custom format flag (dyn.com.rustdesk.owner) to prevent copy-paste loops between host and guest.

name: excel-xml-clipboard-format-special-case
description: Excel exports as XML Spreadsheet format (not standard MIME); clipboard bridges need a branch to preserve Excel fidelity.

name: linux-file-clipboard-unix-only
description: Unix file copy-paste requires X11Clipboard + a local file server; not reachable on Windows by default.

name: audio-device-enumeration-platform-divergence
description: "Audio device enumeration: CPAL (Win/Mac), PulseAudio (Linux non-headless), default fallback."

name: bitrate-calculation-formula
description: base_bitrate = (width × height × fps × 0.07-0.1) / quality_factor; capped per codec.

name: cross-platform-codec-initialization-order
description: "Codec precedence at runtime: hwcodec > vram > software, chosen by probing device capability and user config."

name: display-orientation-rotation-handling
description: Cache RotationMode per display and apply during capture (DXGI, Quartz, X11) — don't re-probe per frame.

name: gdi-fallback-screen-capture
description: On Windows, try DXGI first; fall back to GDI for legacy/virtual displays that DXGI can't enumerate.

name: gop-keyframe-interval-strategy
description: GOP defaults to MAX_GOP (~2-4s) for latency; per-peer config can override.

name: hardware-codec-feature-matrix
description: "hwcodec backend coverage: Intel QSV (Windows/Linux), NVIDIA NVENC (all platforms), AMD AVC/HEVC (Windows), Android MediaCodec."

name: multi-audio-resampling-backends
description: "Resampling backends: Dasp (pure Rust, slow), Rubato (FFT, faster), SampleRate (libsamplerate, fastest)."

name: screen-capture-api-evolution-macos
description: "macOS capture has shifted: legacy Quartz CGDisplayCreateImage → ScreenCaptureKit (Monterey+) behind a feature flag."

name: agent-director-art-director
description: Art Director agent definition — responsibilities, expertise, and decision scope for visual direction

name: agent-director-creative-director
description: Creative Director agent definition — top-level creative authority, vision-keeper role

name: agent-director-producer
description: Producer agent definition — sprint management, milestone tracking, and team coordination

name: agent-director-technical-director
description: Technical Director agent definition — architecture ownership, ADR authority, and technical strategy

name: agent-lead-audio-director
description: Audio Director agent — sound design direction, music, and audio pipeline ownership

name: agent-lead-game-designer
description: Game Designer lead agent — GDD authoring, system design, balance ownership

name: agent-lead-lead-programmer
description: Lead Programmer agent — code review authority, architecture implementation, standard enforcement

name: agent-lead-level-designer
description: Level Designer lead agent — area/level layout, encounter design, flow authoring

name: agent-lead-narrative-director
description: Narrative Director agent — story, dialogue, lore, and world-building ownership

name: agent-lead-qa-lead
description: QA Lead agent — test strategy, coverage tracking, and quality gate authority

name: agent-lead-systems-designer
description: Systems Designer agent — meta-game, progression, economy, and interconnected systems design

name: agent-specialist-ai-programmer
description: AI Programmer specialist agent — NPC AI, pathfinding, behavior trees

name: agent-specialist-gameplay-programmer
description: Gameplay Programmer specialist agent — mechanics implementation, input, game feel

name: agent-specialist-performance-analyst
description: Performance Analyst specialist agent — profiling, budget enforcement, optimization

name: agent-specialist-technical-artist
description: Technical Artist specialist agent — shader authoring, VFX, pipeline optimization

name: agent-specialist-ux-designer
description: UX Designer specialist agent — screen flows, HUD design, accessibility implementation

name: collaborative-design-principles
description: Collaborative design principles for multi-agent game development workflows

name: voxcpm-version-evolution
description: VoxCPM evolved from 0.5B (2-language) → 1.5 (multilingual) → 2 (2B, 30 languages, voice design, 48kHz); versions are not backward-compatible

name: config-system-four-tiers
description: "Four config tiers: Settings (persistent), Local (user), Display (per-display), Built-in (defaults)."

name: langchain-pydantic-memory-curation
description: Prompt-engineering playbook for LLM-based memory extraction from conversations, splitting SYSTEM (facts about the user) from INTERESTING (external wisdom with attribution) with strict quality filters.

name: memory-graph-adaptive-query-with-recency
description: Query agent memory as a DAG, prioritize recency, cap nodes, merge related events

name: skill-solidification-markdown-format
description: Skill format — YAML frontmatter plus markdown body with problem, mechanism, reuse sections

name: validation-report-format
description: Structured validation report — test results, failure analysis, performance metrics, recommendations

name: deb-icon-size-windows-exe-index-mapping
description: Windows `.exe` files pack multiple icon sizes by embedded index; to produce FDO hicolor icons for a Linux package, map each target size (16, 24, 32, 48, 64, 256) to its corresponding image index in the exe using `icoutils` (`wrestool`/`icotool`).

name: actuator-endpoints-skip-auth-by-design
description: In a monitoring collector, target-level BASIC/BEARER auth is stored but intentionally NOT applied when calling /actuator/health and /actuator/metrics.

name: admin-role-disabled-secretkey
description: Default `admins` role is disabled and the shared `SecretKey` was removed from `config.properties` — never re-enable without a per-install key

name: agent-custom-args-blocked-flags
description: User-configured custom_args get a deny-list filter for protocol-critical flags only, not every dangerous flag — workspace members are trusted but not trusted to break the wire protocol.

name: alarm-raw-over-1min-aggregate
description: Alarm judgement reads raw metrics, not 1-minute aggregates — accuracy over storage cost

name: always-pr-branch-never-direct-push
description: "Always create a feature branch and PR for shared repos — never push directly to main, even for 'obvious' additions"

name: assume-semantics-for-compiler-hints
description: Three-tier assertion strategy (Assert / CHECK_NONFATAL / Assume) separates fatal invariants from recoverable logic bugs and pure compiler hints.

name: avro-private-fields-no-setters
description: Avro-generated classes are configured with `fieldVisibility=PRIVATE` and `createSetters=false` to enforce immutability on event payloads

name: base-offset-filename-as-ordering-source-of-truth
description: Encode segment base offset in the filename so directory listing alone reconstructs ordering after crash.

name: cache-even-when-enable-false
description: Notification configs with `enable=false` are still loaded into the cache manager rather than skipped, so toggling enable on/off doesn't require a cache reload

name: canvas-trail-fade-vs-clear
description: Use alpha-rect overpaint instead of clearRect for motion-trail visuals; faster and looks better.

name: categories-array-for-performance-tracing
description: To get a trace that DevTools' TraceEngine can parse with full insights, you must start `page.tracing.start` with the same category array DevTools uses internally — missing a `disabled-by-default-devtools.*` category silently drops insights you'd otherwise get.

name: caveats-absence-confidence-cap
description: "반증 조건(Counter/Caveats)이 비어 있는 knowledge는 confidence 최대 medium으로 강등한다 — 미확인 = 과신 위험"

name: circular-dependency-detection-script
description: A small Python script that parses #include directives across the tree and reports the shortest cycle in the module graph — cheap CI gate against architectural decay.

name: cli-release-accompanies-prod-deploy
description: Every production deployment of the server must be accompanied by a matching CLI release, so server-CLI protocol changes never ship skewed.

name: cmm-namespace-reuse-before-new-key
description: lucida-ui i18n 신규 키를 만들기 전에 `cmm.*` 공용 네임스페이스의 기존 키를 grep 으로 먼저 재사용한다

name: confid-only-query-over-resource-tuple
description: Performance queries key off `confId` (config id) whenever available, falling back to (resourceType, resourceName) only when confId is absent

name: cors-allow-credentials-star-origin
description: 서비스 레벨 allowCredentials=true + allowedOriginPatterns("*") 유지 — 엣지(Traefik/Istio)에서 CORS를 제어하는 아키텍처 전제

name: csp-frame-ancestors-required
description: Send a Content-Security-Policy header (including `frame-ancestors`) on every response to block clickjacking

name: dashboard-decoration-vs-evidence
description: When designing monitoring dashboards for automation pipelines, prioritize visible evidence of system behavior over decorative animations

name: dataavro-json-wrapper-pattern
description: 여러 이벤트 타입마다 Avro 스키마를 만들지 않고 DataAvro{jsonData,jsonDataClass} 하나로 통일 — TCM 선례 채택

name: desktop-route-categories
description: Every path in a tab-based Electron desktop app falls into exactly one of three categories — session routes, transition flows (overlays), or error/stale states (auto-healed).

name: directory-rename-is-atomic-not-file-rename
description: When committing a multi-file JIT cache entry (`kernel.cu` + `kernel.cubin` + optional `.ptx`/`.sass`), rename the *co...

name: do-not-ask-planners-for-api-design
description: Product planners own screens and UX, not endpoint shapes — push API design to AI extraction + developer review instead of template-driven planner input

name: drawer-over-modal
description: Decision to use side drawers (463 files) instead of centered modals (10 usages) as the primary overlay pattern

name: english-only-comments
description: All code comments in English only — keeps the codebase accessible to contributors regardless of native language and plays well with LLM-assisted review.

name: existing-code-patterns-over-standard-rules
description: When generating into an established repo, mirror the repo's existing style first; apply the written standard only when the repo is empty or silent

name: experimental-structured-content-flag
description: MCP structured-content support is gated behind an `experimentalStructuredContent` flag because not all MCP clients parse the field and including it unconditionally can crash older clients; ship it as opt-in during the rollout window.

name: fifth-normal-form-join-dependency-tradeoff
description: 5NF catches join dependencies that 4NF/BCNF miss, but in practice you rarely reason about it directly — design from business entities, recognize AB-BC-AC triangles vs ABC+D stars, and let 5NF fall out.

name: flat-vs-categorized-folder-structure
description: When a content folder crosses ~100 entries, flat structure becomes unnavigable — categorize into ~15–30 semantic buckets even if the tool doesn't require it

name: god-controller-split-by-resource
description: "Split an oversized controller along URL sub-resource boundaries (one controller per sub-path like /page, /widget, /file) and keep the common @RequestMapping prefix — clearer per-class responsibility without breaking the external API surface."

name: hermes-prompt-caching-invariants
description: What must not change mid-conversation to keep Anthropic prompt caching valid.

name: hermes-testing-wrapper-ci-parity
description: Why a shell wrapper around pytest prevents 5 concrete sources of local-vs-CI test drift.

name: hermes-tool-schema-no-cross-toolset-references
description: Why tool schemas must never hardcode other tool names — unavailable tools cause model hallucination.

name: high-confidence-mode-default
description: PredictionMode.HIGH_CONFIDENCE is the default — favoring precision over recall.

name: hub-soft-delete-via-frontmatter-flag
description: "Skills-hub retires low-value entries via an `archived: true` frontmatter flag — never via file deletion. Installers and sync commands skip archived entries; git history remains intact for auditability and un-archive."

name: hyperloglog-for-unique-user-counting
description: Scouter uses HyperLogLog (Clearspring stream-lib) to estimate recent and daily unique user counts with ~100 bytes of memory and ~2% error, avoiding O(n) hash sets

name: hyphenated-root-routes-avoid
description: Never add hyphenated word-group root routes like /new-workspace — they collide with common user workspace names and force endless reserved-slug audits.

name: jacoco-exclude-business-layers
description: In this project's Jacoco config, nearly every layer (service, repository, dto, entity, kafka, config, constants, helper, exception, schedule, provider) is excluded from coverage measurement — only controllers/business orchestration code is measured.

name: jacoco-sonar-exclusion-policy
description: Single exclusion list drives Jacoco + Sonar + test excludes; generated/boilerplate code never counts toward coverage

name: jacoco-sonar-exclusion-rationale
description: Classes under config/, dto/, entity/, kafka/, avro/, exception/, advice/, schedule/, service/websocket/, and generated Q-classes are excluded from Jacoco coverage gates. Only business logic (routers, guards, services) is measured.

name: java-21-upgrade-rationale
description: Java 17 → 21 + Spring Boot 3.5.x upgrade rationale and required Gradle/JVM flag adjustments

name: jest-coverage-80-threshold
description: Global Jest coverage threshold is 80% on branches, functions, lines, and statements

name: js-npm-package-for-web-not-standalone-cli
description: The npm package is for browser/Node library use; the Rust CLI is recommended for command-line work.

name: json-clone-reducer-state-constraint
description: `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(state))` is sufficient for pure-reducer immutability only when state is JSON-safe — it silently drops `Date`, `Map`, `Set`, `RegExp`, functions, and `undefined`.

name: jwt-stateless-refresh-24h-default
description: JWT stateless auth with 1h access / 24h refresh token defaults, both env-tunable.

name: k-kafka-message-header-metadata
description: "멀티테넌트 Kafka routing 메타는 payload 대신 RecordHeader에 두어 business schema 오염을 막는다"

name: kafka-batch-size-timeout-tuning
description: Lowering Spring Kafka max-poll-records (e.g. 200→10) on heavy-processing consumers prevents max.poll.interval.ms timeouts and rebalance storms

name: large-range-query-raw-collections
description: For long time-range analytics over day-partitioned data, query the daily raw collections directly rather than going through an aggregated view to avoid query timeouts.

name: less-antd-theme-only
description: LESS files (71 total) and less-loader exist solely for Ant Design modifyVars theming — LESS is not a general-purpose styling choice in this project

name: license-apply-window-15d
description: Issued licenses must be applied within 15 days; expired licenses are immutable.

name: lighthouse-audit-scope-excludes-performance
description: The project's `lighthouse_audit` tool explicitly runs only the `accessibility`, `seo`, and `best-practices` categories; Lighthouse performance is delegated to `performance_start_trace` because the DevTools TraceEngine gives better Core Web Vitals insights for an agent workflow.

name: mcp-design-principles-for-agent-tools
description: "Seven design principles that make MCP tool servers usable by LLM agents: agent-agnostic protocol, token-optimized returns, small deterministic primitives, self-healing errors, dual human+machine output, progressive complexity, and reference-over-value for heavy assets."

name: measurement-batch-send-per-config
description: Send one bundled Kafka message per target per tick containing all metrics, instead of one message per (target, metric).

name: merkle-tree-range-leaf-vs-point-leaf-tradeoff
description: Whether Merkle leaves should hash individual keys or key ranges determines whether divergence localization scales with dataset size or with divergence size.

name: monorepo-subpath-exports-over-barrel-index
description: T3Code uses explicit subpath exports (e.g., `@t3tools/shared/git`) instead of a single `index.ts` to reduce bundle size and improve tree-shaking

name: no-backwards-compat-pre-launch
description: Before a product is live, prefer removing old code paths over preserving old-plus-new behavior — avoid compat layers, dual-write logic, legacy adapters, and temporary shims.

name: node-npm-pin-matches-jenkins
description: UI build is pinned to Node 24.6.0 + npm 11.5.1 to match the Jenkins build environment

name: non-destructive-curation-for-llm-drafts
description: For pipelines that auto-generate hundreds of LLM drafts, prefer reversible ratings (👍/⭐/👎) with persistent state over immediate deletion; expose a single explicit "Delete 👎" batch action for destructive cleanup.

name: num-waves-1-is-multicast-veto
description: On SM90, if a candidate layout results in exactly one wave of work across the SMs (`num_waves ≤ 1`), DeepGEMM explici...

name: numpy-unpinned-no-workaround
description: Magika unpinned numpy because pip's resolver already handles Python-version compatibility automatically.

name: nvcc-12-9-auto-ffma-interleaving
description: Earlier DeepGEMM versions shipped a SASS-level post-optimizer that interleaved `FFMA` instructions in the compiled cu...

name: opt-out-usage-stats-with-watchdog-subprocess
description: Chrome DevTools MCP implements opt-out telemetry by spawning a detached watchdog child, shipping events to Clearcut via a batched buffer, and gating the whole subsystem on `--no-usage-statistics` (or env var) checked before the telemetry logger is even constructed.

name: optimistic-mutations-by-default
description: Mutations apply the change locally, send the request, roll back on failure, invalidate on settle. The user never waits for the server.

name: organization-soft-delete-grace-period
description: Organization deletion uses a 30-day grace period before permanent removal (DELETE_ORGANIZATION_INTERVAL_DAYS).

name: parallel-dispatch-useful-output-gate
description: The correct pre-flight gate for parallel subagent dispatch is the absolute count of files that actually need work, not the coverage fraction — parallel wall-clock savings dominate until useful_output is near zero

name: personal-access-token-prefix-routing
description: Personal access tokens use a distinct prefix (mul_) so server code can route "is this a PAT or a JWT?" by a cheap string check before parsing.

name: phi-accrual-failure-detector-over-binary-timeout
description: Use phi-accrual suspicion score instead of binary alive/dead for heartbeat visualizations

name: planning-doc-in-issue-description
description: For AI pipelines that read planning docs from issue trackers, favor putting the full markdown in the issue description over wiki-link indirection

name: plugin-repo-scope-generic-skills-only
description: "nkia-skills 같은 공용 플러그인에는 범용 스킬만. 프로젝트 특화 스킬은 각 프로젝트의 `.claude/skills/`에 둔다."

name: postgres-version-rolling-support
description: Roll PostgreSQL major-version support forward incrementally (9.x → 18) rather than pinning one LTS

name: powersync-backend-minimal-indexes
description: For PowerSync-backed Postgres, the backend schema is intentionally **minimal**:

name: powersync-deploy-order-two-pr-invariant
description: For every new PowerSync-synced table, the frontend **must not** ship before PowerSync Cloud's dashboard sync rules ma...

name: prefer-static-import-over-constant-inheritance
description: "Access shared constants via `import static SomeConstants.*` instead of `extends SomeConstants` — inheritance for constants pollutes the single-inheritance slot, leaks protected visibility, and makes subclasses nonsensically \"is-a\" a constants bag."

name: process-title-for-debug-visibility
description: Setting `process.title` early in each process (CLI, daemon, MCP server, watchdog) gives `ps`/`top`/Task Manager immediately-recognizable names — critical when a single install spawns four cooperating node processes.

name: push-local-removed-use-doc-update
description: pushLocalChangesIntoRemote and its action-mapping helpers were removed; mutate Yorkie via doc.update directly

name: query-manager-version-conversion-fallback
description: Unknown target RDBMS versions fall back to a documented default bucket rather than erroring, keeping collection alive

name: rcp-desktop-client-design-rationale-vs-saas-web
description: Scouter chose an Eclipse RCP desktop client over a browser UI to enable high-throughput binary streams, local caching, and real-time drill-down without HTTP/JSON overhead

name: recoil-over-redux
description: Decision to use Recoil atoms (2697 usages, 53 store files) as sole state management instead of Redux in a 25-remote MFA monorepo

name: redact-network-headers-by-default
description: "Network request formatters default to redacting headers (`redactNetworkHeaders: true`) because cookies, Authorization, API keys live there; users opt into raw headers explicitly for debugging auth flows."

name: resource-mount-unmount-use-jar-defaults
description: Don't bind-mount i18n/exception/menu resource files into Spring Boot containers; let the jar ship its own defaults

name: save-child-only-when-changed
description: When reconciling parent→child configurations, persistence is conditional on "new" or "resourceName/tags changed". Unconditional save caused unnecessary write load and spurious change events.

name: scatter-topic-1min-retention
description: Per-tenant scatter-plot/summary topics use 60s retention; metric-history topics use the cluster default. Class-of-data drives retention, not tenant.

name: semantic-design-tokens-only
description: Use semantic design tokens (bg-background, text-muted-foreground) for all styling — never hardcoded Tailwind colors (text-red-500, bg-gray-100) in view code.

name: shadcn-base-ui-not-radix
description: This monorepo uses shadcn with the Base UI variant (@base-ui/react) not Radix — different primitive library under the same shadcn developer experience.

name: shared-postgres-one-db-per-checkout
description: All worktrees share one PostgreSQL container; isolation happens at the database level, not via separate Compose projects.

name: single-file-html-for-file-protocol
description: Generate single-file HTML apps (inline CSS+JS) to avoid CORS errors when opened via file:// protocol

name: single-model-onnx-not-multiple-backends
description: Magika standardized on one ONNX model rather than maintaining separate per-language backends.

name: skill-registry-vs-skill-config-split
description: "원격 저장소 정보는 `.claude/skill-registry.yaml`, 스킬별 런타임 옵션(출력 경로 등)은 `.claude/skill-config.yaml`. 파일 단위로 관심사 분리."

name: skip-implicit-fetch-on-hotpath
description: "Don't `git fetch` (or any network call) implicitly on every CLI invocation. Move staleness detection to an explicit `doctor`/`status` command, and expose `--refresh` as opt-in. Network on the hot path is the single biggest contributor to perceived CLI slowness."

name: squash-merge-orphans-release-branch-tags
description: When a tag is created on a release branch and the PR is squash-merged to main, the tag points at a commit that is no longer reachable from main. Decision - re-tag at the squash commit on main (force-push) to keep tags discoverable.

name: stub-in-service-unblocks-fe-parallelism
description: Emit stub return values inside generated Service methods (tagged `// STUB:`) so FE can connect to a live BE without waiting on business logic

name: symlog-for-lag-metrics-near-zero
description: Use symlog (not log) for consumer lag axes so healthy near-zero state stays visible alongside million-message spikes.

name: system-notification-shared-with-2fa-and-password-find
description: The `system_notification` config (SMTP/SMS credentials) is intentionally shared between 2FA and password-find flows rather than duplicated per flow

name: tabular-metric-excluded-from-list
description: TABULAR measurement type is intentionally excluded from searchable metric-definition list endpoints

name: tag-values-separate-collection
description: TagValueInfo's `values` array was extracted from the parent tag document into a separate `tag_value_detail` collection to bound document size and index cost.

name: tests-follow-code-not-app
description: Tests live next to the code, not the app. If a test mocks next/navigation to test a shared component, the test is in the wrong package.

name: tomcat-request-timeout-1h-for-oz-report
description: Report-generation services should raise Tomcat connection/read timeout to ~1 hour because export of large OZ/Jasper reports is genuinely long-running

name: transpile-packages-over-publish
description: Use transpilePackages in Next.js (and equivalent in Vite) to consume raw-source monorepo packages directly — no build step, no publish step, no dist.

name: triple-styling-paradigm-coexistence
description: Why Tailwind CSS, SCSS modules, and styled-components coexist in the same codebase — migration path, isolation needs, and legacy constraints

name: typed-wikilinks-for-llm-knowledge-base
description: Flat `[[wikilinks]]` carry one bit of information. For an LLM-facing knowledge base, tag every link with a relationship type (supersedes, contradicts, causes, supports) so the graph is queryable rather than just traversable.

name: udp-for-throughput-tcp-for-reliability
description: Scouter streams periodic metrics over UDP (lossy but cheap) and reserves TCP for config fetch, agent registration, and XLog profile uploads that demand delivery guarantees

name: ui-chrome-vs-platform-chrome-split
description: UX affordances live in shared views (web + desktop render identical content); platform chrome (drag strip, immersive mode, tab system) lives in desktop-only code. Violating this split always produces platform divergence.

name: union-merge-catalog-first-seen-wins
description: "When union-merging catalog JSON across N PR branches, use first-seen-wins per slug to preserve install metadata"

name: use-label-not-mime-type-in-workflows
description: Use Magika's stable label field (not MIME type or human description) for automation.

name: variable-length-integer-encoding-for-metrics-bandwidth
description: Scouter's DataOutputX uses 3-byte (INT3) and 5-byte (LONG5) integer encodings to shrink UDP metric packs by 20-40% when most values fit within 24 / 40 bits

name: verification-code-dev-master-888888
description: Dev/self-host stacks may enable a fixed master verification code for evaluation, but this MUST be disabled on public deployments — anyone who knows an email can log in.

name: vllm-kv-cache-fp8-with-mtp
description: vLLM serving chose fp8_e4m3 KV cache + prefix caching + MTP speculative decoding for Qwen3.5 throughput

name: workspace-slug-in-url-refactor
description: Make the URL the single source of truth for multi-tenant workspace context — not a header, not a store, not localStorage.

name: ws-heartbeat-ratio-ping-period
description: WebSocket server ping period should be 90% of pong-wait so a missing pong is detected before the next ping is due (e.g. 54s ping / 60s pongWait).

name: binary-spherical-quantization-bsq-overview
description: BSQ replaces a learned VQ-VAE codebook with sign-quantization on a unit sphere — no codebook parameters, straight-through gradients, and a differentiable entropy regularizer.

name: delivery-mode-streaming-vs-buffered-affects-ui-responsiveness
description: Providers can deliver assistant text incrementally (streaming) or all at once (buffered); affects message timeline UX

name: hierarchical-token-split-s1-s2-high-low-bits
description: Kronos splits each BSQ code into high bits (s1, coarse token) and low bits (s2, fine token) so the LM uses two small softmaxes instead of one huge one.

name: provider-adapter-contract-abstraction-over-codex
description: T3 Code defines a ProviderAdapter interface so multiple agent backends (Codex, Claude, OpenCode) can be swapped

name: no-formatting-only-changes-rule
description: No automatic code formatters on merge; keep diffs semantic-only unless explicit reformat is requested.

name: dockerfile-server-design-choices
description: Why Dockerfile.server uses oven/bun:1.3-slim, creates a non-root craftagents user, chmod's with find -not -perm, and ships the CJS-bundled MCP helpers — each choice solved a real production issue.

name: electron-builder-per-platform-filtering
description: "electron-builder.yml uses per-platform extraResources + files: exclusions to ship only the target's Bun/Codex/Copilot binaries and the matching ripgrep vendor — trimming installer size by 60-80%."

name: http-health-check-binary-takeover
description: Bind the daemon's health port early (before resolving auth or syncing state) to detect another instance already running and prevent two daemons from claiming the same tasks.

name: make-ts-go-verification-pipeline
description: Single make check that runs TypeScript typecheck + unit tests + Go tests + Playwright E2E, starting services only if they're not already running.

name: oss-sync-trademark-boundary
description: The craft-agents-oss repo is the Apache-2.0 subset of an internal repo; oss-sync script and TRADEMARK.md define the legal boundary — code is open, 'Craft' and 'Craft Agents' names are trademarked, afterPack hooks and Pi agent bits are optionally stripped.

name: pgvector-pg17-for-ai-native-apps
description: For AI-native apps that need both relational data and semantic search, use the pgvector/pgvector:pg17 image — one database, no extra infra.

name: release-auto-update-publish-url
description: electron-updater pulls update manifests from https://agents.craft.do/electron/latest — a generic static URL provider, not GitHub Releases or S3 — so Craft Docs Ltd. controls rollout without electron-builder's built-in providers.

name: sway-sampling-timestep-adjustment
description: Sway sampling adjusts the ODE timestep trajectory via a cosine perturbation; coef=1.0 is neutral, tuning 0.8–1.2 affects quality

name: a11y-uid-tool-targeting-over-selectors
description: For agent-driven browser automation, targeting DOM elements by short server-assigned UIDs emitted in an a11y-tree snapshot is dramatically more reliable than asking the agent to construct CSS selectors or XPath.

name: account-lockout-configurable-threshold
description: Failed-login counter locks the account after a configurable threshold (ACCOUNT_LOCKOUT, default 10).

name: agent-runtime-workspace-triple
description: The registration unit on the server is the (agent provider × runtime/daemon × workspace) triple — one daemon running on one machine registers one runtime per (provider × watched workspace).

name: autopilots-and-triggers-model
description: Autopilots are scheduled automations that dispatch agent tasks (create an issue or run an agent directly); triggers are the scheduling layer (cron now, webhook/api future).

name: content-types-knowledge-base-vs-model-outputs
description: content_types_kb.min.json is a research superset — distinct from any single model's actual output space.

name: encrypted-pii-decode-on-export
description: PII fields (phone, email) are stored encrypted; only decrypted for explicit export/API response, never for logs.

name: google-production-scale-hundreds-billions
description: Magika runs at Google scale, processing hundreds of billions of files weekly across Gmail, Drive, and Safe Browsing.

name: hermes-terminal-backends-catalog
description: Six terminal backends an LLM agent can run commands in — local, docker, ssh, modal, daytona, singularity.

name: issue-task-run-hierarchy
description: Issue → Tasks (a.k.a. runs) → Messages. One issue can have many runs; each run streams messages during agent execution.

name: kronos-foundation-model-for-kline-data
description: Kronos is an open-source decoder-only foundation model trained on OHLCV candlesticks from 45+ global exchanges for financial time-series forecasting.

name: mcp-client-name-detection-for-telemetry
description: Detect which MCP client connected (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, Antigravity, OpenClaw, your own CLI) by lowercasing `server.getClientVersion().name` and matching substrings on `oninitialized`, then tag telemetry with that enum for per-client analytics.

name: model-output-space-vs-tool-output-space
description: The 'model output space' (216 NN labels in standard_v3_3) is a strict subset of the 'tool output space' (model outputs + 5 generic labels).

name: model-trained-on-100m-samples
description: Magika's standard models are trained on ~100M files spanning 200+ content types.

name: mongodb-exclude-system-databases
description: When enumerating tenant databases in a shared MongoDB cluster, filter out admin/config/local plus third-party DBs (yorkie-meta, migration) to avoid touching shared state

name: mysql-mariadb-performance-schema-detection
description: MySQL/MariaDB metric availability depends on per-instance Performance Schema state; detect at runtime, don't assume

name: one-default-template-per-type-rule
description: Exactly one default message template may exist per notification type; creating a second default is rejected, but editing the existing default is allowed

name: packed-fp4-as-int8-aliasing
description: DeepGEMM stores FP4 (4-bit float, E2M1 format) tensors under `torch.int8` because:

name: period-interval-naming-convention
description: Period.Mode names encode both unit and multiplier (MIN_15, MONTH_2); monthly modes densify at 24h, not the unit itself

name: pims-write-requires-dryrun-confirm
description: "PIMS 시간 기록/제출/일괄 갱신 등 모든 쓰기 API는 미리보기 → 사용자 확인 → 재시도 가능한 실행 플로우를 반드시 거친다. `--dry-run`은 API 쓰기 호출 자체가 금지."

name: repository-identity-vs-project-vs-thread
description: A RepositoryIdentity is a logical grouping (e.g., for UI); a Project is environment-local with a workspace root; a Thread is a conversation within a project

name: research-paper-published-icse-2025
description: Magika's design and evaluation are published at ICSE 2025 (IEEE/ACM International Conference on Software Engineering).

name: root-span-error-propagation
description: A trace's root-span isError flag must aggregate descendant span errors; otherwise scatter and list views disagree about which traces errored.

name: topresource-immediate-generation-invariant
description: In report immediate-generation, the topResource flag decides whether to query by resource-id (fast path) or the full conf-id set (slow path)

name: turn-quiesced-meaning-follow-up-work-settled
description: A turn is quiesced when initial request completes AND all async work (checkpointing, diffing, reactions) is done

name: virustotal-abuse-ch-integration
description: Magika is integrated with VirusTotal and abuse.ch for file-type detection in malware analysis workflows.

name: workspace-repo-cache
description: Daemon maintains a per-host repo cache so multiple tasks on the same workspace share a warm clone; new tasks create git worktrees off the cache instead of re-cloning.

name: electron-app-resource-path-resolution
description: Electron derives `process.resourcesPath` from `/proc/self/exe`; when the binary and app resources live in separate directories (Nix, custom installs), it points at the wrong tree. A preload wrapper that overrides it using `__dirname` corrects this before any app code runs.

name: electron-preload-wrapper-proxy-interception
description: Electron's module exports use non-configurable property descriptors, so you cannot directly reassign `module.BrowserWindow`; instead, intercept `require('electron')` and return a `Proxy` that overrides only the properties you need.

name: electron-singleton-lock-race-condition
description: Electron's `requestSingleInstanceLock` uses a LevelDB-backed symlink with a PID target; if the previous process exited uncleanly, the symlink remains pointing at a dead PID, causing subsequent launches to silently quit without any error message.

name: menu-bar-visibility-modes-linux-workaround
description: Electron's `autoHideMenuBar` on Linux causes layout shifts on Alt keypress in DEs like KDE Plasma; a three-mode env var (`auto`/`visible`/`hidden`) with boolean aliases gives users control, and `hidden` mode suppresses the Alt toggle by re-hiding on every `show` event.

name: minified-electron-regex-patching-gotchas
description: Minified variable names in Electron bundles change with every release; patches must use optional-whitespace regexes with the `-E` flag and test against both minified and beautified spacing to remain stable across updates.

name: minified-js-ast-anchor-extraction
description: When patching minified JavaScript you cannot rely on line numbers or variable names; instead anchor on stable string literals (error messages, log strings, property names) and extract surrounding minified variable names via regex capture groups.

name: engine-reference-readme
description: Overview of the CCGS engine reference library covering Godot, Unity, and Unreal

name: godot-animation
description: Godot engine reference for the animation module — APIs, patterns, and best practices

name: godot-audio
description: Godot engine reference for the audio module — APIs, patterns, and best practices

name: godot-best-practices
description: Current Godot best practices for CCGS projects — patterns and idioms to follow

name: godot-breaking-changes
description: Godot breaking changes log for the pinned version used in CCGS projects

name: godot-deprecated-apis
description: Deprecated Godot APIs to avoid in CCGS projects with recommended replacements

name: godot-input
description: Godot engine reference for the input module — APIs, patterns, and best practices

name: godot-navigation
description: Godot engine reference for the navigation module — APIs, patterns, and best practices

name: godot-networking
description: Godot engine reference for the networking module — APIs, patterns, and best practices

name: godot-physics
description: Godot engine reference for the physics module — APIs, patterns, and best practices

name: godot-rendering
